subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following cannot be used for measurement of time?
options: Shadow of an object during the day.
answer_explanation: The blinking of eyes is not a regular periodic event with a constant time interval, so it cannot be used as a reliable standard for measuring time.

subject: Science
question_text: Two clocks A and B are shown in Figure 13.1. Clock A has an hour and a minute hand, whereas clock B has an hour hand, minute hand as well as a second hand. Which of the following statement is correct for these clocks?
options: A time interval of 30 seconds can be measured by clock A.
A time interval of 30 seconds cannot be measured by clock B.
Time interval of 5 minutes can be measured by both A and B.
Time interval of 4 minutes 10 seconds can be measured by clock A.
answer_key: Time interval of 5 minutes can be measured by both A and B.
answer_explanation: Clock A doesn't have a second hand, so it cannot measure 30 seconds or 4 mins 10 seconds accurately. Both clocks have minute hands, so both can measure 5 minutes.

subject: Science
question_text: A bus travels 54 km in 90 minutes. The speed of the bus is
answer_explanation: Distance = 54 km = 54000 m. Time = 90 mins = 90 x 60 = 5400 s. Speed = Distance/Time = 54000/5400 = 10 m/s.

subject: Science
question_text: Observe Figure 13.3. The time period of a simple pendulum is the time taken by it to travel from
options: A to B and back to A.
O to A, A to B and B to A.
B to A, A to B and B to O.
answer_key: A to B and back to A.
answer_explanation: The time period is defined as the time taken to complete one full oscillation, e.g., starting from extreme position A, going to B, and returning to A.

question_text: Fig. 13.4 shows an oscillating pendulum. Time taken by the bob to move from A to C is t1 and from C to O is t2. The time period of this simple pendulum is
answer_explanation: The time to move from A to O is t1+t2, which represents 1/4th of a full oscillation. Therefore, the total time period is 4(t1+t2).

subject: Science
question_text: Boojho walks to his school which is at a distance of 3 km from his home in 30 minutes. On reaching he finds that the school is closed and comes back by a bicycle with his friend and reaches home in 20 minutes. His average speed in km/h is
answer_explanation: Total distance = 3 + 3 = 6 km. Total time = 30 + 20 = 50 minutes = 50/60 hours = 5/6 hours. Average speed = Total Distance / Total time = 6 / (5/6) = 36/5 = 7.2 km/h.

question_text: Paheli and Boojho have to cover different distances to reach their school but they take the same time to reach the school. What can you say about their speed?
answer_key: Their speeds are different. Since Speed = Distance / Time, and their distances are different while the time is the same, the one who covers a larger distance has a higher speed.
answer_explanation: Relating speed intuitively.

subject: Science
question_text: The average age of children of Class VII is 12 years and 3 months. Express this age in seconds.
answer_key: 12 years = 12 x 365 = 4380 days. 3 months = approx 90 days. Total days = 4470 days. Total hours = 4470 x 24 = 107280 hours. Total seconds = 107280 x 3600 = 386208000 seconds. (Exact calculation depends on month/leap year conventions, but generally replacing 1 yr with 365 days and 1 month with 30 days is acceptable: 147 months = 147 * 30 * 24 * 3600 = 381,024,000s or using 365.25 etc).
answer_explanation: Unit conversion of time.
provenance_excerpt: The average age of children of Class VII is 12 years and 3 months. Express this age in seconds.

subject: Science
question_text: A spaceship travels 36,000 km in one hour. Express its speed in km/s.
answer_key: Speed = 36000 km / 1 hr. 1 hr = 3600 seconds. Speed = 36000 / 3600 = 10 km/s.
answer_explanation: Unit conversion of speed.

subject: Science
question_text:
Given below as Figure 13.8 is the distance-time graph of the motion an object.
(i) What will be the position of the object at 20s?
(ii) What will be the distance travelled by the object in 12s?
(iii) What is the average speed of the object?
answer_key:
(i) At 20s, the position is 8 m from origin.
(ii) At 12s, the distance travelled is roughly 6 m (depending on exact graph reading).
(iii) Average speed = Total distance / Total time. E.g., if at 20s distance is 8m, speed = 8/20 = 0.4 m/s.
answer_explanation: Interpreting complex distance-time graphs.

subject: Science
question_text: Distance between Bholu’s and Golu’s house is 9 km. Bholu has to attend Golu’s birthday party at 7 o’clock. He started from his home at 6 o’clock on his bicycle and covered a distance of 6 km in 40 minutes. At that point he met Chintu and he spoke to him for 5 minutes and reached Golu’s birthday party at 7 o’clock. With what speed did he cover the second part of the journey? Calculate his average speed for the entire journey.
answer_key: Total distance to cover remaining = 9 - 6 = 3 km. Time remaining = 60 mins - (40+5) mins = 15 mins. Speed for second part = 3 km / 15 mins = 3 / (15/60) hr = 12 km/h. Average speed = Total Distance / Total Time = 9 km / 1 hour = 9 km/h.
answer_explanation: Complex multi-stage speed calculation.

subject: Science
question_text:
Boojho goes to the football ground to play football. The distance-time graph of his journey from his home to the ground is given as Figure 13.9.
(a) What does the graph between point B and C indicate about the motion of Boojho?
(b) Is the motion between 0 to 4 minutes uniform or non-uniform?
(c) What is his speed between 8 and 12 minutes of his journey?
answer_key:
(a) The graph is a horizontal line parallel to the time axis between B and C. This indicates that distance is not changing with time; hence, Boojho is at rest.
(b) Uniform. The graph is a straight line.
(c) Distance travelled between 8 to 12 mins = 225m - 150m = 75m. Time taken = 12 - 8 = 4 mins = 240 seconds. Speed = 75/240 m/s = 0.3125 m/s (or 75m/4min = 18.75 m/min).
answer_explanation: Detailed analysis of a multi-stage distance-time graph.
